summary: The optimal tooth selection varies based on specific requirements but generally ranges from six to eighteen; however, a commonly recommended amount is twelve as it provides an effective balance between thoroughness and feasibility within routine assessments according to current best practices. To ensure comprehensive yet manageable evaluation during personal hygiene performance modifications, clinicians should consider selecting approximately ten to fifteen representative teeth that encompass all quadrants while avoiding unnecessary complexity or oversimplification. This recommendation aims at achieving both accuracy and efficiency without compromising patient care quality significantly under most circumstances observed clinically thus far. Agent2 concludes by emphasizing the importance of balancing comprehensiveness with manageability when choosing which teeth to score using the PHP-M method, suggesting around eleven to fourteen teeth per quadrant would be appropriate for standard use cases involving oral health assessment procedures related directly toward enhancing individualized self-care regimens among patients presenting varying degrees of autonomy over their daily routines concerning cleanliness habits maintenance efforts aimed primarily improving overall wellness outcomes through improved mouth cleaning techniques
